ING W W A—

l. ing JET Verb W 3IfdH 2figT e ; — come + ing = coming, take + ing = taking, drive

+ ing = driving. Exception - be + ing = being

2. ing IT Verb Mii I ee, oe ye k T ; — agree + ing = agreeing, shoe + ing = shoeing, eye + ing = eyeing, see + ing = seeing.

3. ing IT Verb HigT ie y k ; — die + ing = dying, lie + ing = lying

3IIkP 3f y T ; — hurry + ing = hurrying

4. ing M Verb M Vowel Hit M 1

w, r y ; — sit + ing = sitting, win +ing = winning, put + ing =

putting. Except s ' n—pardoning, ironing, opening, burning

5. ITT c Yerb k ing Rt c k ¡ — mimic + ing = mimicking.

VERBS NOT USED IN CONTINUOUS TENSES

1. Verbs of Perception ( ) : see, hear, smell, taste, feel.

2. Verbs of Appearance ) : look, resemble, seem, appear.

3. Verbs of Emotion ( Test ) : want, desire, wish, hate, hope, like, refuse, believe, doubt, mind, care , detest, adore, appreciate , admire, fear, expect.

4. Verbs of Thinking ) think, suppose, agree, consider, trust, remember, forget, know, understand, perceive, realise, recollect.

5. Verbs of Possession ) : belong, owe, own, possess, contain, have.
